subject: ap-chemistry
title: Chemical Equilibrium & Le Chatelier's Principle
stimulus:
2. In the gas phase, AlCl is a molecular substance. A reaction of gaseous AlCl at high temperature is represented
by the following balanced equation.
Reaction 1: AlCl (g) → Al(g) + 3Cl(g) ΔH  = ?
3 1

parts.prompt:
A potential energy diagram for Cl is shown in the following graph.
(i) Based on the graph, what is the bond length, in picometers, for Cl ? _________
(ii) A student finds that the average Al − Cl bond length is 220 picometers and the average bond energy is
425 kJ/mol. Draw the potential energy curve for the average Al − Cl bond on the preceding graph.
rubric_criteria: (ii) For a curve with a minimum at an internuclear distance of 220 ±10 pm: 1 point
For a curve with a minimum energy value of −425± 20 kJ/mol that approaches zero as the 1 point

parts.prompt:
Three proposed Lewis diagrams for the AlCl (g) molecule are shown.
(i) The AlCl (g) molecule has a trigonal planar geometry. Which diagram (1, 2, or 3) can be eliminated
based on geometry? Justify your choice based on VSEPR theory.

(ii) Which of the three diagrams is the best representation for the bonding in AlCl ? Justify your choice
based on formal charges.
